title: Sum of values in subitems column reflected in parent item column
excerpt:
I’d love to have the sum of subitems in a number column be reflected in the parent item of the main column. 
Example: I use the number column to list out budgets for projects, and sometimes there are subitems for certain…

posts.author: shdelahunt (Sean Delahunt)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’d love to have the sum of subitems in a number column be reflected in the parent item of the main column.

Example: I use the number column to list out budgets for projects, and sometimes there are subitems for certain projects with their own individual budgets. I’d like the sum of those subitem budgets to be reflected in the parent item column.

There appears to be a paid app Rollup Subitems (monday.com: Where Teams Get Work Done) that should achieve this, but looking for something native to the functionality. Similar question previously listed here as well, so there is a definite demand for this
(Subitem Budget Totals Reflected in Parent Budget Column)

Please upvote and make this happen!

posts.author: oscica (Oscar Icaza)
posts.cookedHtml:
You can use a mirror column in the parent that sums the subtotal of the sub-items, and then just create a grand total with the parent’s budget and the sub-item’s budget.

I hope this option is useful for you.

posts.author: inspiredearth (Jonathan)
posts.cookedHtml:
I had the same need as the OP. But I also need the resulting data to be accessible in Monday automations, and over the API.
Mirror Columns are not accessible in either of those.
What’s the most efficient way to get the SUM into a Number column so that it can be referenced in Monday Automations and in the API?
